Package neqsim.process.safety.rupture

Class FireExposureScenario

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.rupture.FireExposureScenario
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class FireExposureScenario extends Object implements Serializable
Fire exposure definition for trapped-liquid rupture screening.

The scenario converts an industry fire basis, such as an API 521 pool-fire heat input or a fixed incident heat flux, into a heat flux applied to the exposed pipe or flange surface.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • FireExposureScenario

      public FireExposureScenario(String name, FireExposureScenario.FireType fireType, double exposedAreaM2, double heatFluxWPerM2, double environmentalFactor, double emissivity, double viewFactor, double flameTemperatureK, double ambientTemperatureK, double passiveProtectionHeatFluxFactor)
      Creates a fire exposure scenario.
      Parameters:
      name - scenario name
      fireType - fire calculation method
      exposedAreaM2 - exposed external area in m2; must be positive
      heatFluxWPerM2 - fixed heat flux in W/m2 for fixed-flux cases
      environmentalFactor - API 521 environmental factor for pool-fire cases
      emissivity - effective flame emissivity for radiative cases
      viewFactor - view factor for radiative cases
      flameTemperatureK - flame temperature in K for radiative cases
      ambientTemperatureK - ambient temperature in K; must be positive
      passiveProtectionHeatFluxFactor - heat flux multiplier after PFP or insulation, from 0 to 1
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the scenario input is invalid

  • Method Details

    • api521PoolFire

      public static FireExposureScenario api521PoolFire(double exposedAreaM2, double environmentalFactor)
      Creates an API 521 pool-fire exposure.
      Parameters:
      exposedAreaM2 - exposed external area in m2; must be positive
      environmentalFactor - API 521 environmental factor; must be positive
      Returns:
      fire exposure scenario

    • fixedHeatFlux

      public static FireExposureScenario fixedHeatFlux(double exposedAreaM2, double heatFluxWPerM2)
      Creates a fixed heat-flux exposure.
      Parameters:
      exposedAreaM2 - exposed external area in m2; must be positive
      heatFluxWPerM2 - incident heat flux in W/m2; must be positive
      Returns:
      fire exposure scenario

    • radiativeFire

      public static FireExposureScenario radiativeFire(double exposedAreaM2, double emissivity, double viewFactor, double flameTemperatureK, double ambientTemperatureK)
      Creates a radiative fire exposure using Stefan-Boltzmann heat flux.
      Parameters:
      exposedAreaM2 - exposed external area in m2; must be positive
      emissivity - effective flame emissivity from 0 to 1
      viewFactor - geometric view factor from 0 to 1
      flameTemperatureK - flame temperature in K; must be positive
      ambientTemperatureK - ambient temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      fire exposure scenario

    • withPassiveProtectionFactor

      public FireExposureScenario withPassiveProtectionFactor(double heatFluxFactor)
      Returns a copy with a passive-fire-protection heat-flux factor applied.
      Parameters:
      heatFluxFactor - heat flux multiplier from 0 to 1
      Returns:
      protected fire exposure scenario

      public double incidentHeatFlux(double outerSurfaceTemperatureK)
      Calculates incident heat flux into the external wall surface.

      outerSurfaceTemperatureK - current outer wall temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      incident heat flux in W/m2

    • heatInputW

      public double heatInputW(double outerSurfaceTemperatureK)
      Calculates total incident heat input to the exposed area.
      Parameters:
      outerSurfaceTemperatureK - current outer wall temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      heat input in W

      private static void validatePositive(double value, String name)
      Validates that a numeric value is positive and finite.
      Parameters:
      value - value to validate
      name - parameter name used in exception messages
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is invalid
